report: Sarah Reyes as Sparring Partner — Playtest (deterministic core-model + static audit)
system: Sarah comms thread, five presentation-only surfaces: (1) Analyst's Bet 2.0, (2) confidence calibration + later callback, (3) two-voice stakeholder moment, (4) mentor trails, (5) end-of-mission performance mirror + carry-over perk
scope: This is a DETERMINISTIC CORE-MODEL playtest plus a static source audit, not a live in-browser click-through. Every surface runs through sarah-sparring-core.js — the same pure logic career-sim.js calls at runtime (extracted behavior-preservingly, mirroring consequence-core.js) — so the bet/calibration/two-voice/trail/recap MECHANICS are exercised exactly, but the production DOM render+delegation flow (renderEvidencePanel / renderDebrief / click handlers) is verified by reading career-sim.js, not by driving the UI. Interactive e2e is unavailable this session and the screenshot tool is static.
method: Each surface is driven across ALL FOUR missions with the REAL authored SARAH_CONTENT. The performance mirror sweeps seven posture profiles covering every reinforced strength and every improvement nudge. A scoped static invariant audit of career-sim.js confirms no grade chrome in any generated string and that the sparring layer is presentation-only: no graded write, no direct localStorage write, NO new saveCareerState (perk is a session-scoped module var), and no confidence stacking.

bets.prompt: Before this release decision goes up the chain — suppose your read is wrong. What would you expect to find if it were?
Before you write up the unknown device — suppose your read is wrong. What would you expect to see if it were?
Before you escalate this account activity — suppose your read is wrong. What would you expect to see if it were?
This one goes to incident response — suppose your read is wrong. What would you expect to see if it were?

calibration.prompt: Before you commit — how sure are you about this release call, and why?

callback.withFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Now that the payment record is on the table, does that still hold?
callback.withoutFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Does the latest finding change that?

calibration.prompt: Before you commit — how confident is your read on this device, and why?

callback.withFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Now that the payment record is on the table, does that still hold?
callback.withoutFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Does the latest finding change that?

calibration.prompt: Before you escalate — how sure are you about this compromise read, and why?

callback.withFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Now that the payment record is on the table, does that still hold?
callback.withoutFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Does the latest finding change that?

calibration.prompt: Before IR takes it — how confident is your exfiltration read, and why?

callback.withFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Now that the payment record is on the table, does that still hold?
callback.withoutFinding: Earlier you put your confidence at Medium — “two records look like live PII”. Does the latest finding change that?

twoVoice.reconcile: Both pressures are real, and neither is yours to settle. Your job is to put what the evidence shows in front of them so they can weigh it — name what you see, and let them own the call.
Each is protecting something worth protecting — uptime and containment. You do not have to choose for them; lay out what the device is doing and let the evidence frame the trade-off.

One voice fears disruption, the other fears a breach — both are reasonable from where they stand. Your role is to surface what the sessions actually show so the call is made on evidence, not on whoever is loudest.

Continuity and containment are both real stakes, and the call above your pay grade. Give them the evidence trail you built — what moved, where it went, from which endpoint — and let them weigh it with eyes open.

mirror.label: all-strong
mirror.strength: You put a number on your confidence and said why. Calibrating out loud is a senior habit — keep doing it.
mirror.nudge: Push one finding past the point it feels necessary — the extra step is where the real story usually hides.

mirror.label: calibrated-but-no-unknowns
mirror.strength: You put a number on your confidence and said why. Calibrating out loud is a senior habit — keep doing it.
mirror.nudge: Try naming what you DON'T know out loud — flag an Unknown. An explicit gap sharpens the whole team's focus.

perk.label: Open-question carry-over
perk.note: Next case, the first Unknown you flag earns a quick Scope Snapshot recap.

mirror.label: calibrated-but-narrow
mirror.strength: You put a number on your confidence and said why. Calibrating out loud is a senior habit — keep doing it.
mirror.nudge: Cast a wider net next time — pull from more than one tier of evidence before you commit your read.

perk.label: Wide-net carry-over
perk.note: Next case, Sarah will nudge you to corroborate across tiers before the call.

mirror.label: consistent-no-calibration
mirror.strength: You logged a clear call on finding after finding instead of hedging. That decisiveness on the record is exactly what I lean on in a partner.
mirror.nudge: Next case, put a number on your confidence and say why. Calibrating early catches over-reach before it costs you.

perk.label: Calibration carry-over
perk.note: Sarah will prompt you to calibrate the moment your read firms up next case.

mirror.label: broad-no-calibration
mirror.strength: You pulled signal from every tier of the evidence, not just the loud parts. That breadth is how a lead reads a case.
mirror.nudge: Next case, put a number on your confidence and say why. Calibrating early catches over-reach before it costs you.

perk.label: Calibration carry-over
perk.note: Sarah will prompt you to calibrate the moment your read firms up next case.

mirror.label: falsifiable-bet-only
mirror.strength: You committed to a read and named what would prove you wrong. Stating a falsifiable position takes real analyst nerve.
mirror.nudge: Next case, put a number on your confidence and say why. Calibrating early catches over-reach before it costs you.

perk.label: Calibration carry-over
perk.note: Sarah will prompt you to calibrate the moment your read firms up next case.

mirror.label: bare-engagement
mirror.strength: You worked the evidence before you called it. Leading with what you can see is the whole job — nicely done.
mirror.nudge: Next case, put a number on your confidence and say why. Calibrating early catches over-reach before it costs you.

perk.label: Calibration carry-over
perk.note: Sarah will prompt you to calibrate the moment your read firms up next case.

noGradeChromeInSparringLayer.note: Audited every player-facing string the sparring layer produces or ships across all four missions: bet prompts/labels/coaching, calibration prompts + generated callbacks, two-voice who/stance/reconcile, mentor-trail lines, and recap strength/nudge/perk copy. No ✓/✗, no correct/incorrect/right/wrong verdict language. The bet label never states the verdict; naming a disconfirming criterion is not the answer.

presentationOnlyNoNewWrites.note: sarah-sparring-core.js is pure (no DOM/storage/window/grading). The sparring layer in career-sim.js never calls setDiscoveryJudgment (the SOLE graded writer), never writes localStorage directly, and adds NO new saveCareerState — the carry-over perk lives in a session-scoped module var (SARAH_SESSION_PERK) that is consumed in openCareerMission, never serialized. It also never touches confSpend / activateScopeSnapshot / BET_STAKE, so the trail's scope recap cannot double-charge confidence, and it reads NO keyed-correctness helper (the performance mirror keys on posture: committed calls, breadth, calibration, unknowns, falsifiable bet) so the debrief can never become a hidden grade. Trails arm inside setDiscoveryJudgment behind try/catch (best-effort).

screenshots.note: Per-surface interactive screenshots require a live click-through, which is unavailable this session. An in-mission Analyst Notebook view was captured statically and reviewed for a clean boot; the sparring sections render from the same renderEvidencePanel path exercised here.

notes.observation: The two-voice reconciliation is choice-INDEPENDENT by construction (twoVoiceReconcile ignores choiceId), so neither stakeholder can read as the 'right' pick. The performance mirror reads posture only and ignores an injected score, so it cannot become a hidden grade.
notes.impact: None — this is the intended design and is asserted directly.
